---
title: "Bottleneck 1"
author: "Jacob Bauer"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: word_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
# This function takes a population and randomly removes a number of observations from it. The number of observations removed will be no less than 10% and no more than 90%. 
# Inputs: population size
# Outputs: decreased population size in a data frame
remove_random_pct <- function(population) {
  n <- length(population) # Gets the size of the population
  
  pct <- runif(1, min = 0.1, max = 0.9) # Randomly generates a number between 0.1 and 0.9
  
  num_remove <- floor(n * pct) # Calculates the number of observations to remove from the population size
  remove_indices <- sample(seq_len(n), size = num_remove) # Creates the random "alive" or "death" in the population
  
  result <- population[-remove_indices] # Removes the observations considered "dead"
  
  final_result <- data.frame(result = result, pct_removed = pct) # Creates a data frame of the new population and percent that was removed from the population
  
  return(final_result)
}
```


```{r}
pop <- 1:1000

bottle_neck <- remove_random_pct(pop)
```
